ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: M le sum niak fu abe m basi ate fi ŋaaŋsaŋa a piisi ase chiŋmarisaŋa ale bo wenŋmazukku ale tintain busuŋ ale bo beli po la. Fi ŋaaŋsaŋa ale tuesi ba dachaasaŋa tɛŋsaŋa a tara.
translated: When the sun rises, I go to the garden to pick some vegetables and bring them back home to cook, and after eating, I go to the forest to hunt, and when I return, I rest.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: Abrayam yaa weeni ayen, <<M nyɔnɔ ni dan nya ku magsi ayen ni jam m yenni, ni kan gaam ni yɔmawa taamu.
translated: Abraham was old, and the Lord had blessed him in every way, and he had become very wealthy.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: M le basi ate m nɔiyeŋka a chim nɔiyeŋ kaai ale kaa kpeglim mi ale fi ale fi ŋaaŋbisaŋa suŋsuŋ la. Ka le kasim boro kama ale fi ŋaaŋ sii ale cheen la. M le chim fi Wen ale fi ŋaaŋsaŋa Wen.
translated: The dog is very clever and can catch a monkey and bring it to its owner, and the owner will give it food as a reward. When it catches a monkey, it will bring it to its owner. The dog belongs to its owner.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Adam jam paari ka bena kobsaneok ale pisita alege kpi.
translated: Adam is going to the garden to harvest the ripe areca nuts.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Jekob yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a saalim fu, wi fu yuei a sak mu.>> Alege wa tulisi wa ayen, <<Ka boan nyiŋ ate fi a bek mi yuei ya?>> Ate wa yaa niak wa dula.
translated: Jacob said to his mother, "I will go and hunt, and I will bring you meat." His mother said to him, "Where will you go and hunt to bring me meat?" And he said to her.
